title: Vandellia setulosa
taxonomy: Vandellia setulosa is classified under the kingdom Plantae, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plants. Vandellia setulosa further falls under the order Lamiales, a group of flowering plants known for their bilaterally symmetrical flowers. The species is also known by its other common names, but its classification remains well-defined within the botanical community.

intro: Vandellia setulosa, commonly known as the "Indian blood root," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, known for its medicinal properties and traditional uses. The health benefits of Vandellia setulosa include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
